app.title: Datasets Deputy
app.subtitle: Composite dataset manager
app.statusScope: MVP: UI, file management, manual annotation

menu.aboutBody: Dataset image preview, annotation dimension management, and export tools.
menu.author: Aeka
menu.developmentNotice: Development build: features are incomplete and errors may occur.

batchEdit.smartSeparatorDescription: Complete a comma and space at the join only when needed

annotationFormatConversion.title: Annotation format conversion
annotationFormatConversion.selectFormatsHint: Please select the current format and target format.
annotationFormatConversion.descriptionGeneric: The available settings depend on the selected current and target formats.
annotationFormatConversion.descriptionBooruTagToAnima: Convert standard Booru Tag annotations to Anima format, typically by adding @ before style tags and adding dedicated quality words.
annotationFormatConversion.descriptionAnimaToAnima: Adjust Anima quality words while keeping the annotation format unchanged.
annotationFormatConversion.descriptionAnimaToBooruTag: Remove Anima-specific annotation formatting, typically including @ before style tags and dedicated quality words.
annotationFormatConversion.descriptionBooruTagToNaturalLanguage: Convert standard Booru Tag annotations to natural language. This conversion relies on an LLM.
annotationFormatConversion.descriptionNaturalLanguageToBooruTag: Convert natural language to standard Booru Tag annotations. This conversion relies on an LLM.
annotationFormatConversion.descriptionAnimaToNaturalLanguage: Convert Anima-format annotations to natural language. This conversion relies on an LLM.
annotationFormatConversion.descriptionNaturalLanguageToAnima: Convert natural language to Anima-format annotations. This conversion relies on an LLM.
annotationFormatConversion.descriptionNaturalLanguageRewrite: Rewrite natural language annotations with an LLM. XML batching can merge multiple rows into one request.
annotationFormatConversion.placeholderLineOne: Convert annotations in the current training set for different annotation formats.
annotationFormatConversion.placeholderLineTwo: Some conversions require LLM API access, and conversion quality depends on the model.
annotationFormatConversion.lowQualityModelWarning: Models with poor output quality may cause annotation conversion to fail.
annotationFormatConversion.stepOneTitle: Step 1 - Add prefix to style tags
annotationFormatConversion.stepOneDescription: An @ will be added before every style tag.
annotationFormatConversion.reverseStepTwoDescription: Find quality words and remove them.
annotationFormatConversion.reverseStepThreeDescription: Remove isolated punctuation left behind by the conversion.
annotationFormatConversion.llmPromptPlaceholder: Describe how the annotations should be rewritten...
annotationFormatConversion.nlRewriteStepThreeXml: The returned XML will be parsed and restored to the matching annotation rows.
annotationFormatConversion.nlRewriteStepThreeDisabled: XML batching is off, so each response is written directly to its annotation row.

annotationNormalization.title: Batch normalize annotations
annotationNormalization.description: Process all annotations under the current annotation profile in the current dataset.

formatValidator.title: Image format validator
formatValidator.noFolderSelected: Please select a folder containing images
formatValidator.hint: Select a folder and click "Run scan" to check whether image file extensions match their actual format.
formatValidator.noMismatches: All image extensions match their actual format. No issues found.
formatValidator.fixComplete: Done. Renamed {{count}} file(s) to correct extensions.

trainingCacheCleaner.title: Remove trainer cache
trainingCacheCleaner.noFolderSelected: Please select a dataset or training output folder
trainingCacheCleaner.hint: Select a folder to scan for .npz files, _latent_cache folders, and .aitk_size.json files.
trainingCacheCleaner.noItems: No trainer cache files were found in this folder.

duplicateSimilarity.title: Duplicate/similar image detection
duplicateSimilarity.noFolderSelected: Please select a folder containing images
duplicateSimilarity.hint: Select a folder and run CLIP-based duplicate/similar detection. Results are report-only.
duplicateSimilarity.noGroups: No duplicate or similar groups were found.

tree.consolidateLooseFilesDescription: Create a sibling folder and move {{count}} loose images into it.
tree.confirmDeleteLooseFilesDescription: This deletes {{count}} loose images in this level. System files and child folders are not touched.
tree.hint: Select a dataset folder to show its image grid on the right.
tree.confirmDescription: This removes the image index and annotations for this dataset from the app database. Local files will not be deleted.
tree.confirmDescriptionSuffix: for this dataset from the app database. Local files will not be deleted.
tree.confirmAssetDescription: This removes the image index and annotations for this asset database. Imported asset files are not cleaned up automatically yet.
tree.confirmAssetDescriptionSuffix: for this asset database. Imported asset files are not cleaned up automatically yet.
tree.confirmFolderDescription: This only removes the folder path from the tree. Local images and TXT annotation files will not be deleted.
tree.confirmDeleteWorkspaceSubfolderDescripti: This permanently deletes the subfolder and all images, annotations, instructions, and other files under it. This cannot be undone.
the subfolder and all images, annotations, instructions, and other files under it
tree.deletedDatabaseTrainingSet: The complete image index for this training set, plus {{count}} edited annotation types and instructions.
tree.deletedDatabaseSubfolder: The image index for this subfolder, plus {{count}} edited annotation types and instructions.
tree.deletedAssetTrainingSet: The complete image index for this training set, the image copies in the asset library, plus {{count}} edited annotation types and instructions.
tree.deletedAssetSubfolder: The image index for this subfolder, the image copies in the asset library, plus {{count}} edited annotation types and instructions.
tree.deletedFolderMount: The workspace folder mount entry in the left tree.
tree.deletedWorkspaceSubfolder: This subfolder and all images, annotations, instructions, and other files under it.
tree.keptDatabase: The source images, annotations, and instruction files at the import paths.
tree.keptAssetDatabase: Source images, annotations, and instruction files at the original import paths. Asset files referenced by other databases are also kept.
tree.keptFolderMount: Local images, annotations, and instruction files.
tree.keptWorkspaceSubfolder: Other content in the same workspace folder that is outside this subfolder.

folderImport.title: Confirm image import
folderImport.databaseNote: Only adds database index entries; files are not moved or copied.
folderImport.assetNote: Images are copied into the app asset library; the original path is kept as an import record.
folderImport.noProfiles: No annotation types available for this dataset.
folderImport.unsupported: Image import is only supported for dataset subfolders.
folderImport.previewDone: Image import preview complete: {{imageCount}} images selected, {{annotationCount}} annotations and {{instructionCount}} instructions detected.
folderImport.importDone: Image import complete: {{imported}} imported, {{skipped}} skipped, {{failed}} failed; {{annotationCount}} annotations, {{instructionCount}} instructions.

workspace.name: Name
workspace.deleteAnnotationTypeDescription: This removes all annotations and instructions of this type across all images. This cannot be undone.
workspace.filterHiddenHint: Change View - Filter view to browse hidden items

itemMenu.deleteDescription: This deletes the item's image, annotation, and instruction together. This cannot be undone.
itemMenu.deleteDescriptionScope: the item's image, annotation, and instruction
itemMenu.deletedDatabaseImage: The software image index for this item, plus {{count}} edited annotation types and instructions under it.
itemMenu.deletedAssetImage: The software image index for this item, the image copy in the asset library, plus {{count}} edited annotation types and instructions under it.
itemMenu.deletedFolderImage: The local image file, TXT annotation file, and .inst.txt instruction file.
itemMenu.keptDatabaseImage: The source image, annotation, and instruction files at the import path.
itemMenu.keptAssetImage: Source image, annotation, and instruction files at the original import path. Asset files referenced by other databases are also kept.
itemMenu.keptFolderImage: Other images, annotations, and instruction files in the same folder.

settings.title: Settings
settings.uiAnimationDescription: Controls UI transition effects such as the sidebar, tree expand/collapse, and view switching.
settings.highlightCellStateDescription: Use color in table view to mark unsaved changes and recently saved content.
settings.autoSaveAfterAnnotationDescription: Save generated annotations automatically after an annotation run completes.
settings.autoSaveAfterBatchDescription: Save batch add and batch replace changes automatically after they are applied.
settings.thumbnailSizeDescription: Used when generating new thumbnail cache files. Clear the thumbnail cache manually to regenerate existing thumbnails.
settings.thumbnailCacheDescription: Shows current thumbnail cache usage and can clear it to free local disk space.
settings.logFilesDescription: Shows current log file usage and can clear it to free local disk space.
settings.wd14ModelPathPlaceholder: Select a WD14 model folder containing ONNX or PyTorch weights
settings.clipModelPathPlaceholder: Select a local Hugging Face CLIP model folder
settings.geminiApiDescription: Configure Gemini communication endpoint and model.
settings.networkImageTransferDescription: Control image resize and format conversion before sending images. Only used for temporary API requests and does not affect local files.
settings.geminiImageResizeDescription: Resize images before sending API requests. This can significantly control token usage and reduce errors, but excessive compression will lose detail. Images smaller than the preset size are not compressed.
settings.geminiImageFormatDescription: Convert images to the selected format before sending. This can reduce traffic usage and improve network communication efficiency, but it does not save token usage.
settings.openAiApiDescription: Configure OpenAI, OpenRouter, vLLM, or another /v1 compatible endpoint.
settings.openAiApiKeyPlaceholder: Enter API key, or leave blank for local endpoints
settings.anthropicApiDescription: Configure Claude Messages API communication endpoint and model.
settings.grokApiDescription: Configure xAI Grok through its OpenAI-compatible /v1 endpoint.
settings.doubaoApiDescription: Configure ByteDance Doubao through Volcengine Ark's OpenAI-compatible endpoint.
settings.llmLoaderDescription: Configure local or online LLM backend links. Empty links use localhost with each loader's default port.
settings.pythonEnvDescription: Configure PyTorch and ONNX Runtime for local neural network models. The app can reuse runtimes from apps like ComfyUI, or install its own runtime dependencies when needed.
settings.pythonEnvExternalPlaceholder: Select an environment folder or app root
settings.pythonEnvExternalHint: Supports common paths such as a ComfyUI root, ComfyUI/python_embeded, venv, kohya_ss/venv, and ai-toolkit/venv. Available subfolders are detected automatically.
settings.pythonEnvPathSelected: Reuse folder selected. Run detection to confirm available runtimes.
settings.runtimeManagedSourceDescription: The app prepares its internal environment automatically when PyTorch or ONNX Runtime is installed. You do not need to manage Python separately.
settings.runtimeManagedPathPending: The internal path will be created automatically on the first runtime install.
settings.pytorchRuntimeDescription: Runs PyTorch models. CUDA 12.8 is recommended, while CUDA 13.0 is available for newer GPUs and drivers.
settings.onnxRuntimeDescription: Runs ONNX models. DirectML is recommended by default on Windows for broader compatibility.
settings.runtimeExternalHint: When reusing another app's existing environment, this app will not modify it. If it is unavailable, install an independent environment.
settings.pythonEnvCudaUnavailable: CUDA was not detected; this environment will use CPU only or lacks GPU support
settings.pythonDepsInstallConfirm: This will automatically prepare the app-managed internal environment and download PyTorch dependencies from the internet. The download can be large. Continue?
settings.onnxDepsInstallConfirm: This will automatically prepare the app-managed internal environment and download ONNX Runtime dependencies from the internet. Continue?
settings.pythonEnvInstalling: Installing PyTorch dependencies. This may take a while...

image.noAnnotations: This image has no annotations yet
image.selectAnnotationHint: Select an annotation to edit, or create a new annotation type

welcome.title: Let's start
welcome.description: Select a dataset project on the left, or import an image folder to start browsing, curating, and manually annotating.

import.title: Importing dataset
import.cancelling: Cancelling and cleaning up imported content...

importWizard.title: How would you like to handle your dataset?
importWizard.assetDatabaseDescription: Copy source files into the app-managed asset library. Source paths are kept as import records, so the library stays usable if originals move or disappear.
importWizard.dynamicDatabaseDescription: Use SQLite to manage image indexes and annotations while still reading images from their original paths. Best when images change frequently and different annotation sets need to be managed for different models.
importWizard.databaseDescription: Use SQLite to manage image indexes and annotations while still reading images from their original paths. Best when images change frequently and different annotation sets need to be managed for different models.
importWizard.folderDescription: Use this app to manage and annotate dataset folders like a native file explorer - all changes are edits to files under your working path.
assetDatabasePoints: Complete dataset storage management based on SQLite
Stores images, multiple annotations, and instructions
Fully independent from imported files and original paths
One image can map to multiple annotation types
Flexible export before training as needed
Static storage, unaffected by external asset changes
dynamicDatabasePoints: Annotations and instructions stored in the database
Images can come from different paths
One image can map to multiple annotation types
Flexible export before training as needed
Images update with source files, annotations are managed by the database
Missing source files affect preview and export
databasePoints: Annotations and instructions stored in the database
Images can come from different paths
One image can map to multiple annotation types
Flexible export before training as needed
Images update with source files, annotations are managed by the database
Missing source files affect preview and export
folderPoints: Annotations and instructions stored as TXT files beside images
The imported path is the only working path
One image maps to a single annotation
The folder is the source of truth - no separate export step
External file changes are reflected dynamically
Removing only drops the working path; files on disk are untouched

table.unsavedCellsWithOther: {{count}} unsaved cells here, plus {{otherCount}} in {{profileCount}} other annotation type(s)
table.otherUnsavedCells: {{count}} unsaved cells in {{profileCount}} other annotation type(s)
table.emptyDescription: Import a folder to start browsing and annotating a dataset.

detail.title: Image details
detail.noSelection: Select an image to edit annotations.

databaseExport.title: Export database
databaseExport.description: Export a single SQLite database, optionally bundled with image files.
databaseExport.databaseOnlyHint: Exports only the .sqlite file. Imported databases will keep using the image paths stored in the database.
databaseExport.withImagesHint: Exports a .zip archive containing database.sqlite and image copies under images/.

databaseImport.title: Import database
databaseImport.description: Import a Datasets Deputy .sqlite database or a .zip archive that includes images.
databaseImport.sqliteHint: Importing a .sqlite file does not copy images; it keeps the image paths stored in the database.
databaseImport.zipHint: Importing a .zip extracts images/ into the target folder and rewrites image paths in the database.
databaseImport.complete: Import complete: database has {{imageCount}} images; copied {{copiedImageCount}} images.

exitGuard.title: Unsaved changes
exitGuard.description: All unsaved changes will be lost. Exit?
